statement: The vNext pilot loop should redefine safe-heal as a single bounded Temporal-native repair action (start/resume/nudge) that operates on workflow binding + projection freshness, and remove any remaining queue-era transport repair semantics from campaign-state interpretation.
rationale: The historical bug ledger and observability backlog were dominated by queue-era concerns (scheduler kickoff, transport requeue, batch/job lifecycle). The Temporal cutover changed the execution substrate, and the engine already exposes Temporal-native stuck reasons and a generic repair mutation. Keeping queue-era semantics in the pilot loop would create false diagnoses and drift the loop away from actual runtime truth.
keyAssumptions: Convex remains a projection store and can safely trigger Temporal start/resume for processes.
A single bounded repair is sufficient to distinguish infra staleness from real engine bugs.
validationCriteria: Campaign reports and stuck summaries use Temporal-native reason codes only.
Safe-heal executes at most one bounded repair per stuck process per iteration and otherwise stops for forensics.
Iteration snapshots capture workflow binding + last projection timestamps to support root-cause analysis.
critiquePoints: Some failures may require multiple interventions (e.g., manual worker restart + resume), but those are infra operations and should not be modeled as repeated agentic heals.
questions: Should the safe-heal action also attempt workflow cancellation for known-bad executions, or remain strictly start/resume-only?
